title: News - Scarlet Fever Bacterium Detected in 700-Year-Old Tooth - Archaeology Magazine
description: News - Scarlet Fever Bacterium Detected in 700-Year-Old Tooth Archaeology Magazine A Bolivian Mummy's Tooth Is Rewriting The History of Scarlet Fever ScienceAlert 700-year-old mummy's tooth reveals ancient disease that killed thousands scarlet fever India Today Scientists were searching for human DNA in a 700-year-old mummy in the Andes but found something unexpected that could change what is known about the origins of respiratory diseases in pre-Columbian America. CPG Click Petróleo e Gás 700-year-old mummy from Bolivia contains earliest confirmed evidence of strep throat bacteria in the Americas Live Science

title: The End Of Oil? As Fuel Shocks Cascade, 53 Nations Gather To Plan A Fossil Fuel Phaseout
description: (MENAFN - The Conversation) US President Donald Trump is a longtime climate denier and oil industry ally, who sums up his own energy policy as–drill, baby, drill–. Yet he is doing more than almost ...

title: Ancient gold helmet returned to Romanian after theft from Dutch museum
description: A 2,500-year-old golden helmet stolen last year from a Dutch museum where it was on loan has been returned to Romania.

title: Ancient Wisdom, Modern Play: Lingxi Games Brings Three Kingdoms’ Culture to the World
description: A viral “becoming Chinese” trend has prompted overseas audiences to engage with Chinese culture in new ways, and Three Kingdoms stories are now at the center of the talk. People are even voting on a classic question: If you were thrown back in time and had to join Cao Cao, Liu Bei or Sun Quan, who would you join?

title: 250-million-year-old fossil provides proof that our mammal ancestors laid eggs
description: The fossil, found in South Africa, belonged to an embryo of the Lystrosaurus.
